target.field_c: Mammalian circadian clock regulation (CLOCK/BMAL1-CRY/PER feedback loop)

disjointness_check.string_verification: Zero interactions between Fe-S proteins (NFS1, CISD2, GLRX5, FDX2) and clock proteins (BMAL1, CRY1, PER2, CLOCK) at any confidence threshold
disjointness_check.kegg_verification: Zero shared genes between hsa04122 (Sulfur relay) and hsa04710 (Circadian rhythm)

key_papers.title: Genes for iron metabolism influence circadian rhythms in Drosophila melanogaster
key_papers.relevance: CRITICAL: NFS1/IscS RNAi disrupts circadian activity in Drosophila clock neurons. 14-year-old with ZERO mammalian follow-up. THE KEY UNEXPLOITED LEAD.

key_papers.title: Diurnal control of IRE-containing mRNAs via IRP1/IRP2
key_papers.relevance: IRP1 is a [4Fe-4S] protein. Diurnal IRE-mRNA control is the downstream readout that validates upstream Fe-S oscillation.

key_papers.title: Glioblastoma stem cells resist cuproptosis with circadian variation of copper levels
key_papers.relevance: NEAREST PRECEDENT: BMAL1 -> ATP7A -> copper -> Fe-S cluster protein destabilization. First mammalian evidence of circadian -> metal -> Fe-S stability. BUT: focuses on cuproptosis/copper, not Fe-S BIOGENESIS machinery.

key_papers.title: NFS1 selective inhibitor Compound 53
key_papers.journal: Int J Mol Sci (Zhu et al.)
key_papers.relevance: First selective NFS1 inhibitor (IC50 = 16.3 uM). TOOL COMPOUND for testing circadian NFS1 hypothesis.

key_papers.title: D-cysteine inhibits NFS1
key_papers.relevance: Second NFS1 inhibitor. D-cysteine as tool compound. Published 2025.

key_papers.title: Peroxiredoxin 24h oxidation cycles
key_papers.relevance: Established 24h peroxiredoxin oxidation cycle conserved across all domains of life. Upstream redox oscillator.

key_papers.title: Circadian NAD+/NADH oscillation in mouse liver
key_papers.relevance: ~30% NAD+/NADH amplitude change over 24h in mouse liver. Input value for Nernst calculation.

literature_context: Fe-S cluster biogenesis x Circadian clock is CONFIRMED DISJOINT in mammalian systems. GLRX5 x circadian = 0 papers. NFS1 x circadian = 1 non-mechanistic paper. STRING shows zero interactions between Fe-S and clock protein networks. The 2012 Drosophila paper (Mandilaras & Missirlis, PMID 22885802) is the ONLY mechanistic evidence: NFS1/IscS RNAi disrupts circadian activity — 14 years with zero mammalian follow-up. The 2026 JCI paper (PMID 41480765) is the nearest mammalian precedent: BMAL1 controls copper homeostasis affecting Fe-S protein stability. Key enablers: (1) NFS1 has two tool compounds published 2025 (Compound 53 and D-cysteine), (2) IRP1 aconitase/IRE-BP switch provides measurable downstream readout validated as diurnal by Nadimpalli 2024, (3) Nernst calculation gives 3.07-fold Kd shift from 30mV redox oscillation, (4) Labile cluster half-lives (1-8h) give 31-94% amplitude tracking of 24h oscillation.

computational_notes.cluster_halflife_check: t_half 2-8h gives 31-80% amplitude tracking of 24h oscillation. PLAUSIBLE.
computational_notes.nfs1_km_check: NFS1 Km ~50uM, [Cys] ~150uM: 17% activity change from +/-30% cysteine. MARGINAL — primary mechanism likely direct redox modification, not substrate.
computational_notes.kegg_overlap: Zero shared genes between Fe-S (hsa04122) and circadian (hsa04710) pathways.
computational_notes.ampk_bridge: AMPK in circadian pathway can indirectly regulate mitochondrial Fe-S via PGC-1alpha. 2-3 steps removed.
computational_notes.compartmentalization: NFS1/ISCU2 are mitochondrial; CRY1/BMAL1 are nuclear. Bridge via mitochondrial redox -> Fe-S assembly -> GLRX5/CIA export to cytoplasm.
